chief_complaint: A 29-year-old male presented with new-onset generalized tonic-clonic seizures and a 44-year-old woman presented with headache, tinnitus, polydipsia, and polyuria.
history: Case 1: A 29-year-old male with new-onset generalized tonic-clonic seizures. Case 2: A 44-year-old woman with longstanding hypothyroidism, headache, tinnitus, polydipsia, and polyuria. Both patients had elevated serum IgG4 levels.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings not provided.
labs: Case 1: Brain MRI revealed a left-sided, extra-axial dural-based enhancing lesion with vasogenic edema. Serum IgG4 was elevated (3.25 gm/dL). Whole body PET-CT ruled out systemic involvement. Case 2: Imaging revealed an enlarged pituitary with systemic FDG-avid lesions on PET-CT. Serum IgG4 was elevated (3.01 gm/L).
